item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: baby don't hurt me

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Full Licensing Information:

The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: lovelove
Author(s): Marion Doss, Yale Rosen, Roget
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Roget as an edited version of the two following images

Source: Flickr
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: gage kiss
Author: Marion Doss

Source: Flickr
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: Mucoepidermoid carcinoma, low grade - Case 199
Author: Yale Rosen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Image credit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"There's a giant slug downstairs that transforms into your greatest sexual desire…"
"Wait it transforms?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The impression I get is that it may be feeding of the emotions and sexual acts it abuses people for.
It's a pretty straightforward and short skip, and I think it works well.

I would like a clarification about the nest part, though, perhaps it could be worded better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The imagery definitely makes this SCP and is enough to warrant a +1. That being said, a lot remains unclear about this thing. Does it really just need a place a to live or is it somehow feeding off it's victims like a parasite? How is it able to maintain a "nest" of so many hotel rooms? Is it literally in multiple places at once? What exactly is the meaning of "nest" in this context? Has it actually built some grotesque. slimy, alien-esque nest that somehow encompasses all of these rooms or is nest just a word used to describe where it lives?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's say 031 identifies 'relationships' chemically, rather than emotionally - the actual chemicals produced in the brain in a relationship.

If it were to work like this, asexuals, aromantics, and people who had never been in relationships before would likely envision their parents.

The brain cannot chemically distinguish familial and romantic relationships - so this creature's effect on people who fit the above requirements would be much, much, more disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *grammatical

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's doctored.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where does an image like that even come from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It appears as someone the subject had a romantic attraction to, not necessarily someone they had a relationship with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congratulations! That image makes me want to gag, and makes this article feel much more disturbing! I will see this in my nightmares!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I asked Roget about it a while back, he wants it to stay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, it was a preventative measure since articles like this were being targeted by trolls for some time.

I'd first check with Roget about the amnestics/amnesiacs change. It may have been a stylistic choice here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
However, wide distribution of amnesiacs

Should probably say "amnestics" by now, but I can't edit it, as the page is locked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does it affect people who aren't aromantic, but have never been in a relationship?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And, personally, I do not believe that there is such a thing as an asexual person.

https://www.asexuality.org/

Edit: Whoops, I missed your subsequent comments in which you addressed this. Sorry.

item.title: So . . .
item.content:encoded: this thing is +300 now, it seems

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spooky and gross, things I do not want to feel while reading a skip.

+1

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Yes, it's the lyrics to the song this SCP title is based off of. Don't do this. Clogs up recent posts and comes off as spammy. That goes to anyone who sees an SCP with song lyrics in the title and thinks "I'll go post the entirety of that song's lyrics on the discussion page!".

This is a closed staff post. Do not reply to this post for any reason if you are not staff.

item.title: Lyrics brother
item.content:encoded:
+ Collapsible
- Collapsible

What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
Baby don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
What is love
Yeah
Oh, I don't know why you're not there
I give you my love, but you don't care
So what is right and what is wrong
Gimme a sign
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
Whoa whoa whoa, oh oh
Whoa whoa whoa, oh oh
I don't know, what can I do
What else can I say, it's up to you
I know we're one, just me and you
I can't go on
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
Whoa whoa whoa, oh oh
Whoa whoa whoa, oh oh
What is love, oh, oh, oh
What is love, oh, oh, oh
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
Don't hurt me
Don't hurt me
I want no other, no other lover
This is our life, our time
When we are together, I need you forever
Is it love?
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more, Yeah (oh, oh)
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
What is love
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more (oh, oh)
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more (oh, oh)
Oh baby, don't hurt me
Don't hurt me no more
What is love?

Collapsing these lyrics. ~ Decibelle

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noting for posterity that psychology does in fact use the terms asexual and aromantic. The biology concept is "asexual reproduction". If anything, that's the one that leans towards jargon; it's a blanket term for a number of different reproductive strategies like parthogenesis, budding, and vegetative cloning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BABY DON'T HURT ME

DON'T HURT ME

NO MORE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feel sorry for the guy in the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Morty, kill Pensylvester for me."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is kind of like the memory parasites from Rick and Morty

item.title: updatish
item.content:encoded: I've gone through and edited this to be less crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NGAHHH!! WHAT'RE YOU LOOKING AT, PUNK?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Say no one was observing it but you. Does it take on the specific personality of the person it is appearing to you as?

Because if so I would run.

…

It's Undyne now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's like me and my girlfriend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The change was made a few months after the post; but aromantic would be the correct term, if SCP-031 is only affecting subjects who experience some kind of romantic attraction at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Asexual" has a biological meaning that probably should take precedence in clinical writing. It specifically refers to methods of reproduction that don't involve the exchange of genes between parents. Any other usage is, frankly, slang or possibly jargon that is easily confused with the jargon of biology. What term does psychology use?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article currently says aromantic, not asexual. Is that an error?

Also, a surprising number of people seem to be *really* invested in that distinction.
wooo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that because this thing exudes romantic attraction as opposed to sexual, that alloromantic asexuals would be affected; rather aromantic subjects would not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I meant asexual. I was referring to people who could have romantic relationships but didn't have any sexual desires. Asexual is a recognized term. Being aromantic != being asexual, after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1. I feel that the article doesn't live up to the picture. Also, the "asexual" thing jars me right out of my willing suspension of disbelief. Aromantic might work there, but if this thing only is interested in couch-surfing, sex shouldn't be involved. Romance != Sex, after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just everytime I come here, I hear this.

-1, I've heard that enough. (However, my downvote was based on the article)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bravo, Bravo, absolutely beautiful I'm in tears!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one humours me, and it's not because it references a song. It's for a much, much more immature reason I cannot and should not detail here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As an asexual person, it's nice to hear an acknowledgment that I'm real every once in a while, especially when some commenters obviously disagree. (Only 1% of the population is asexual, okay, and a lot of them are aromantic too. So let us have our fun.)

+1, although I wish I could upvote it a few more times. (Also, Djoric, your little sing-along about how things should be interesting instead of scary is perfect. I wish I could upvote comments, because to tell you this directly, I'd be commenting on a two-year-old comment, which is bad form.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Responding to a two year old post with an overused and annoying as hell meme? Please do not do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like trains.

item.title: Re: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded:
http://potd.pdnonline.com/2010/10/6964

Notice: This link is not safe for work. Please remember to tag nsfw links in the future. That being said, it's definitely cool stuff so check it out when you're at home.- Scantron

item.title: Re: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded: I know that one day, when I was working on an earlier version of this, yoric came up to me and chat(for this was when yoric was around, that's how long I've worked on this) and said "here you mentioned this article you've been working on I think this image fits". So I kept it and actually the final version of this article is very much influenced by the picture. I want to believe yoric made it, or summat. Maybe I'll have someone ask him one day.

item.title: Just wondering...
item.content:encoded: So does anyone actually know where this picture is from?

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I was just about to post that. Damn you Roget.

item.title: Re: Downvoting the fleshy body pillow
item.content:encoded:
How is that first part funny to you? All I'm getting is irritation from that statement.

This is a fleshy blob that slowly convinces you make out with it. It's fucking gross and creepy, which is why I upvoted it a whole ago, when I first joined.

item.title: Re: Downvoting the fleshy body pillow
item.content:encoded:
I know what I'm replying to is over half a year old, but this seemed like the right place to put this…

I honestly thought for the first half of this that it should be a -J. Like, SCP-031 is that buddy from college that almost everyone has, who's trying to bum off their old "pal", or that annoying family member who never gets a job and just worms their way into the homes of family and never leaves.

I think it has potential, but it's just not doing it for me as it is right now. Others have already said what I'd say, so I won't just copypasta stuff.

Baby don't hurt me

Don't hurt me

Don't hurt me

No more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel that, and truthfully, it would be really easy to add a twist to this. I mean, it basically tricks you into loving it and letting it move in with you. That seems like a lot of effort for free rent…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, this is precisely what I came here to comment on. To what end is it trying to get into the homes and be loved? The reason this skip doesn't really grab me is because it is boring. Whoa, you see it as a love interest of yours. Perception filter. Cool. Now what? It tries to live with you? Why? Is it lonely? Is it trying to lay eggs? Does it want to engage in coitus? What does it get out of the deal, other than a roof over its head?

In a nutshell- needs a twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: actually i was talking about http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camille_(Red_Dwarf)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
- I'm guessing he means Duane Dibbley

It's worth seeing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've actually never seen that show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so… red dwarf reference?

But on a serious note, has testing with children been done? preferably ones who have had no romantic encounters of any kind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's for science. Science has no bounds when it comes to the gross factor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, the smoke is in the shape of SCP-031's normal body, but it surrounds the humanoid figure.

Personally, I'm imagining the Penguin. From Batman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…do not need to know that.

/afk Bleaching my brain

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the addendum it reads

[..] report SCP-031 as being a small, plump humanoid figure obscured by dark smoke in the shape of SCP-031's body

but earlier it was already established that

SCP-031 is an amorphous organism

So it reads to me as if SCP-031 is perceived as both humanoid and shapeless at the same time.
